messages.content: Write dialogue, Molly McGee looks through Andrea Davenport's social media, where she sees Andrea’s new post, but something is odd, she hasn’t posted in a few days, she decides to go to Andrea’s house, where Molly found out that Andrea died in her sleep due to her heart condition, but Molly knew nothing about Andrea having a heart condition until her father and Molly's friend, Ollie Chen explains it (including how her heart condition is incurable, very rare (to the point that it was unnamed), how Andrea and her parents knew she had it for years, and how her heart condition was eventually going to take her anyways), the next day, Molly reads the latest post on Andrea’s social media, explaining her death and how in honour of her memory no more posts will be made on any of her social media and all of her social media accounts will still be available, albeit with the ability to comment or reply disabled, as they can’t monitor all the harmful comments from cyberbullies 24/7, how they managed to legally name Andrea’s heart condition after her and how they have made a foundation in Andrea’s name to raise awareness of heart conditions similar to Andrea’s and to support research on a cure written by her parents out loud

Molly: (Looking through Andrea's social media) Hmm, it's been a few days since Andrea last posted anything. That's unusual. Let me check her latest post.

(Opening the post)

Molly: Wait, what? "In memory of Andrea Davenport"? What does that mean? (Confused)

(Ollie enters the room)

Ollie: Hey, Molly. What are you looking at?

Molly: Ollie, there's something strange going on. Andrea's latest post is about her death, but I had no idea she had any kind of health condition. Did you know about this?

Ollie: (Sighs) Yeah, Molly. Andrea had a heart condition. It was incurable, very rare, to the point that it was unnamed. Her parents knew about it for years.

Molly: (Taken aback) What? Why didn't she ever mention it to me?

Ollie: Andrea didn't want people to pity her or treat her differently because of it. She wanted to live a normal life as much as possible. Her parents respected her wishes.

Molly: But if her condition was so serious, why didn't she seek medical help?

Ollie: Molly, they tried everything. Doctors couldn't figure out a cure for her specific condition. It was progressive, and eventually, it was going to take her life.

Molly: (Sadly) That's so unfair. She was so young and full of life.

Ollie: I know, Molly. It's heartbreaking. Andrea's parents were devastated when she passed away in her sleep. They were always prepared for the inevitable, but it still doesn't make it any easier.

Molly: (Wiping away tears) So, what's with her social media post then? It seems like her parents wrote it.

Ollie: They did. They wanted to honor Andrea's memory and raise awareness about heart conditions like hers. They've even managed to legally name Andrea's heart condition after her. They want people to know that heart conditions can go unnoticed and how important it is to get regular check-ups.

Molly: That's incredible. What about her social media accounts?

Ollie: They'll still be available, but her parents disabled the comments and replies. It was to protect Andrea's memory from cyberbullies. They couldn't handle monitoring harmful comments all the time. But they've created a foundation in Andrea's name to support research for a cure and raise awareness about heart conditions similar to hers.

Molly: That's a beautiful way to keep her memory alive. I want to do something to honor Andrea too.

Ollie: I'm sure Andrea would appreciate that, Molly. She valued friendship and compassion above everything else.

Molly: She truly was an incredible person. Let's find a way to support the foundation and help others with similar conditions.

Ollie: I think that's a wonderful idea, Molly. Andrea would be proud of you.

Molly: Thank you, Ollie. Let's do our best to make sure her memory lives on and continues to inspire others.
What did they name it

Ollie: They named Andrea's heart condition "Davenport Syndrome" in her honor. Her parents wanted her name to be associated with raising awareness and finding a cure for similar heart conditions. It's a fitting tribute to her memory.
Ollie tells Molly that Andrea's parents are both supporting on both a cure and more research about it in general with the foundation, he also tells Molly what the foundation is called

Ollie: Andrea's parents are fully dedicated to supporting research for a cure for Davenport Syndrome and advancing knowledge about heart conditions like hers. The foundation they established is called the "Heartbeat Foundation." They chose this name to symbolize the importance of every heartbeat and to emphasize the need for awareness and support for those affected by rare and incurable heart conditions. They hope to make a lasting impact through this foundation in Andrea's memory.

Ollie then tells Molly that the reason why they support more research about it in general is because no one knows exactly what caused it or what it is, and if there are people outside of Andrea who have it

Ollie: Molly, one of the reasons Andrea's parents are so determined to support more research is because the exact cause and nature of Davenport Syndrome is still unknown. Andrea's case was incredibly rare, and there are no concrete answers about what caused it or the specifics of the condition itself. They believe that by funding research and raising awareness, they can potentially shed more light on this condition and help those who may be suffering from similar heart conditions.

Furthermore, they understand that there might be others out there who have undiagnosed cases of Davenport Syndrome or similar conditions. Through the Heartbeat Foundation, they aim to connect with individuals who might be experiencing similar symptoms or concerns, so they can provide support, knowledge, and potentially identify any patterns that may arise. It's about bringing a sense of hope and understanding to those who are affected, as well as their families and friends.
Molly tells Ollie what will happen to Andrea's body, her parents know she was terrified of ghosts and doesn’t want her afterlife being about her being something she’s scared of, Ollie then answers

Molly: Ollie, what will happen to Andrea's body now? I know this may sound strange, but I remember Andrea telling me once that she was terrified of ghosts. She didn't want her afterlife to be about her being haunted or something she was scared of. I just want to make sure her wishes are respected.

Ollie: I understand your concern, Molly. Andrea's parents have decided to have her cremated. It was a difficult decision for them, but they wanted to honor Andrea's wishes and ensure that her afterlife is not associated with her fears. They believe that by choosing cremation, her spirit can be free and at peace.

Molly: That's a relief. I'm glad they considered Andrea's fears and wishes. It's important to respect her even after she's gone.

Ollie: Absolutely, Molly. Andrea's parents love her deeply and want to ensure that she is remembered and honored in the best possible way. They know how much she valued her peace of mind, and they want to provide her with that even in her afterlife.

Molly: Thank you for reassuring me, Ollie. It's a comfort to know that Andrea's parents are taking care of her in every aspect, even after her passing.

Ollie: Of course, Molly. Andrea was a dear friend to both of us, and her parents are doing everything possible to support her wishes and preserve her memory. They want to focus on the positive impact she had in her life rather than dwelling on the sadness of her loss.

Molly: It's a beautiful way to remember Andrea – celebrating her life and the mark she left on all of us. Let's continue to support her parents and the Heartbeat Foundation in raising awareness and finding a cure for conditions like Davenport Syndrome.

Ollie: Absolutely, Molly. Andrea would be proud of us. Let's honor her memory by making a difference in the lives of others facing similar challenges.
He then tells Molly what they will do with her ashes and her funeral, as she stated many times that she doesn’t want a big normal funeral

Ollie: As for Andrea's ashes, her parents have decided to keep them with them for now. They will be placing her urn in a special place in their home, where they can remember and honor her every day. They want to keep Andrea close to their hearts and make her a part of their lives even in her physical absence.

In terms of the funeral, Andrea had expressed multiple times that she didn't want a big, traditional ceremony. Instead, her parents have chosen to have a small, intimate gathering with close family and friends. They want to create a peaceful and meaningful farewell, focusing on celebrating Andrea's life and the impact she had on all of us.

They have decided to hold the ceremony in a serene location that held a special place in Andrea's heart – a nearby park she loved to visit. The event will be reflective and personal, allowing everyone to share their memories, stories, and words of tribute. It will be a chance for all of us to say our final goodbyes and find solace in the support of one another.

Molly: That sounds perfect. Andrea always preferred simplicity and genuine connections. I'm glad her parents are honoring her wishes and providing a space that reflects who she was.

Ollie: Absolutely, Molly. Andrea was unique in so many ways, and her parents want to ensure that her funeral is a meaningful reflection of her individuality and spirit. They want it to be a comforting and uplifting experience for everyone who loved and cared for her.

Molly: Thank you, Ollie. I appreciate you sharing all of this with me. It's comforting to know that Andrea's parents are making choices that align with her wishes and who she was.

Ollie: You're welcome, Molly. We're all here for each other during this difficult time. Let's continue to support one another and keep Andrea's memory alive through the foundation and the positive impact we can make for others.

He then tells Molly that her parents will decorate her urn with all her favourite things from all her life, including shows she used to watch when she was a toddler to the books she loved just before she died

Ollie: Molly, I have a bit more to share about Andrea's urn. Her parents have decided to decorate it with all of her favorite things from throughout her life. They want to create a beautiful and personalized tribute that truly reflects who she was.

They plan to include items that were significant to her, from the shows she loved as a toddler to the books she cherished just before she passed away. Each item will represent a different phase of her life and the memories she held dear. It will be a visual representation of her unique journey and the things that brought her joy.

By adorning her urn with these meaningful objects, her parents hope to create a cherished remembrance that captures the essence of Andrea's vibrant and colorful existence. It will be a testament to the diverse range of interests and experiences that made her who she was.

Molly: That's a beautiful idea. It's so touching to see how much thought and love Andrea's parents are putting into honoring her memory. It will be a fitting tribute to her life and everything that made her special.

Ollie: Absolutely, Molly. Andrea's parents want her memory to live on in a unique and personal way. They believe that by incorporating her favorite things into the decoration of her urn, they can keep her spirit alive and preserve the essence of who she was.

Molly: It's truly a testament to the love and bond they shared with Andrea. I hope it brings them some comfort in their grieving process.

Ollie: I'm sure it will, Molly. Memories can be incredibly powerful, and these tangible reminders of Andrea's life will serve as a source of comfort and strength for her parents.

Molly: Thank you, Ollie, for sharing all of this with me. As we continue to support Andrea's parents and honor her memory, let's remember the joy she brought into our lives and the impact she made on us and others.

Ollie: Absolutely, Molly. Andrea's spirit will continue to shine through all the lives she touched, and together, we can keep her memory alive while making a positive difference in the world.
What were Andrea's favourite shows when she was a toddler (they don’t exist in real life)

Ollie: Andrea's parents shared with me some of her favorite shows from when she was a toddler. They created these show titles and descriptions to reflect her lively imagination and sense of adventure:

1. "Rainbow Land Adventures": A whimsical animated series where a group of talking animals embark on exciting journeys through a vibrant and magical world full of colorful rainbows and enchanting surprises.

2. "Starry Galaxy Explorers": An animated space-themed show that follows a crew of adorable alien creatures as they explore different planets, discover fascinating life forms, and solve cosmic mysteries.

3. "Dancing with the Silly Monsters": A live-action show where energetic and silly monsters teach kids various dance moves in a fun and interactive way. Each episode features catchy tunes and laughter-filled dance routines.

4. "Imagination Express": A magical train ride that takes children on imaginative adventures to fantastical worlds. Along the way, they meet talking animals, encounter legendary creatures, and learn important life lessons.

These are just a few examples of the imaginative shows Andrea enjoyed during her toddler years. They sparked her creativity and fostered her love for exploration and learning. The titles and descriptions were created to reflect her unique interests and the joy these shows brought to her life.
Molly tells Ollie to not tell her anymore, as she is already crying too much

Molly: Ollie, I appreciate you sharing all of this with me, but I think I've reached my emotional limit for now. Hearing about Andrea's favorite shows from when she was a toddler is just making me cry even more. Please, hold off on telling me any more details for now. I need some time to process everything.

Ollie: I completely understand, Molly. I'm sorry if it brought up more emotions for you. Take all the time you need, and whenever you're ready to talk or have any questions, I'll be here for you. We're all going through this grieving process together, and it's okay to feel overwhelmed at times.

Molly: Thank you for your understanding, Ollie. I know you're only trying to help. I just need a little break to process everything and gather my thoughts. I'll reach out to you when I'm ready to talk again.

Ollie: Take as much time as you need, Molly. Remember, we're here to support each other. Reach out whenever you're ready, and know that we'll get through this together.
Molly wakes up, and realises it’s all a nightmare

Molly: (Awakening from the nightmare, gasping for breath) Oh, thank goodness, it was just a nightmare. Andrea is okay. Everything is okay.

(Molly takes a few moments to calm down and catch her breath, realizing it was all a terrifying dream.)

Molly: (Talking to herself) Wow, that was intense. Andrea is safe, and there's no need to worry. I should reach out to her, though, just to reassure myself.

(Molly grabs her phone and quickly dials Andrea's number, relieved when she answers.)

Andrea: Hey, Molly! What's up?

Molly: Andrea, it's so good to hear your voice. I had a really vivid nightmare... about you. I just needed to make sure you're okay.

Andrea: Oh, Molly, I'm perfectly fine. Don't worry. It was just a dream. You know how our minds can play tricks on us.

Molly: Yeah, you're right. Sorry for freaking out like that. I'm so relieved to know you're okay. I don't know what I would do without you.

Andrea: Aw, Molly, you're the best. I'm here for you too. Nightmares can be really unsettling, but remember, I'm just a phone call away. If you need someone to talk to, I'm here.

Molly: Thank you, Andrea. I appreciate it. I'm feeling much better now. It was just a dream, after all. Let's catch up soon, though. I missed you.

Andrea: Absolutely! Let's plan something fun. We definitely need to catch up. I missed you too, Molly.

(Molly hangs up the phone, feeling a sense of relief wash over her. She takes a deep breath, grateful that it was all just a nightmare and that her dear friend Andrea is safe and sound.)

Molly: (Smiling) Time to live in the present and make the most of the moments we have. And no more scary dreams, please!
